pairs.question: Who was Elmer John Gedeon and what significant accomplishments did he achieve in both sports and military service?
pairs.answer: Elmer John Gedeon was an American professional baseball player and United States Army Air Forces officer. He played briefly for the Washington Senators in Major League Baseball in 1939 and was an All-American track and field athlete at the University of Michigan, where he tied a world record in the high hurdles in 1938. Gedeon also earned varsity letters in football and baseball during his college years. He later served as a bomber pilot in World War II and was posthumously recognized for his bravery after being shot down and killed over France in 1944.

pairs.question: What notable records or achievements did Elmer Gedeon accomplish in track and field during his college career?
pairs.answer: During his time at the University of Michigan, Elmer Gedeon tied a world record in the high hurdles in 1938. He was also named an All-American in track and field, showcasing his exceptional athletic talent.

pairs.question: How did Elmer Gedeon's baseball career progress after graduating from college?
pairs.answer: After graduating from the University of Michigan, Elmer Gedeon played in the minor leagues for most of the 1939 and 1940 baseball seasons. He was called up to the Washington Senators briefly in September 1939, marking his only stint in Major League Baseball.

pairs.question: What military honors or recognitions did Elmer Gedeon receive during World War II?
pairs.answer: Elmer Gedeon was decorated for bravery after his plane crashed during a training flight in 1942. He was later killed in action while piloting a Martin B-26 Marauder on a combat mission over France in April 1944. Alongside Harry O'Neill, he is one of only two Major League Baseball players killed during World War II.

pairs.question: Which MLB team did Elmer Gedeon play for, and in what years did he appear in games?
pairs.answer: Elmer Gedeon played for the Washington Senators in Major League Baseball. He appeared in several games for the team in 1939.

pairs.question: What was Elmer Gedeon's role in the United States Army Air Forces during World War II?
pairs.answer: Elmer Gedeon served as a bomber pilot in the United States Army Air Forces during World War II. He trained as a pilot and flew several missions in the European Theater of Operations before being shot down and killed over France in 1944.
